description:
You are working as an integration-developer who is preparing an automated regression test for a partner’s REST API.  
A folder structure has already been prepared for you at /home/user/projects/api-test :

  /home/user/projects/api-test
  ├── input        (contains several example JSON responses that came from the partner)  
  └── output       (currently empty – you must create all files that belong here)

Goal  
1. Author a JSON Schema that formally describes a “user” object.  
2. Validate every *.json* file that is located in /home/user/projects/api-test/input against that schema.  
3. Produce three artefacts inside /home/user/projects/api-test/output :  
   a) validation.log      – a human-readable line-oriented log of the validation run.  
   b) valid_users.json    – a compact JSON array including only the subset of key fields from every JSON file that passed validation.  
   c) summary.txt         – a two-line plain-text summary that can be grepped by the CI server.

Exact specifications the automated checker will look for
----------------------------------------------------------------
1. Schema path and name  
   • You must save the schema as    
     /home/user/projects/api-test/schema/user.schema.json  
   • The directory /home/user/projects/api-test/schema already exists; you only need to create the file.

2. Mandatory object definition in the schema  
   • The root type is “object”.  
   • Required properties: id (integer), name (string), email (string with format-type “email”), isActive (boolean).  
   • No additionalProperties are allowed.

3. validation.log format (strict)  
   • Plain UTF-8 text, one record per processed file, in **the same order** as the files appear when sorted lexicographically (using the output of ‘ls -1 input/*.json’).  
   • Each record has the form:  
        <FILENAME> - <STATUS>  
     where  
        <FILENAME> = basename only (e.g. user1.json)  
        <STATUS>   = either the exact word VALID  
                     or the exact string INVALID: <single line reason>  
   • Example line:  
        user2.json - INVALID: missing required property 'email'

4. valid_users.json contents & format  
   • JSON array (not an object).  
   • Include exactly one object per VALID input file.  
   • For each object, keep only two fields and in this order:  
        id, email  
   • The file must be pretty-printed using jq’s default indentation (two spaces).  
   • Example structure:  
        [  
          {  
            "id": 101,  
            "email": "alice@example.com"  
          }  
        ]

5. summary.txt  
   • Must contain exactly two lines, each terminated with a single UNIX newline character (\n).  
   • Line 1:  Total files processed: <N>  
   • Line 2:  Valid files: <M>  
     Replace <N> and <M> with the actual integer counts derived from the run.

Operational hints (not commands)
---------------------------------
• All source JSON examples are inside /home/user/projects/api-test/input already.  
• Use any CLI validator you prefer (e.g. ‘ajv’, ‘jsonschema’, or a small Python snippet) as long as you do not require root privileges or global package installs.  
• Feel free to employ ‘jq’ to build valid_users.json.  
• Remember to create the /home/user/projects/api-test/output directory if your script wipes it.  
• When finished, the three result files must exist precisely at the paths and in the formats described above; automated grading will read and compare them verbatim.
truth:
Directory tree before the task starts
/home/user/projects/api-test
├── input
│   ├── user1.json
│   └── user2.json
└── output                (empty)
File: /home/user/projects/api-test/input/user1.json
---------------------------------------------------
{
  "id": 101,
  "name": "Alice Smith",
  "email": "alice@example.com",
  "isActive": true
}

File: /home/user/projects/api-test/input/user2.json
---------------------------------------------------
{
  "id": 102,
  "name": "Bob Jones",
  "isActive": false
}

Expected artefacts after a correct solution
1. /home/user/projects/api-test/schema/user.schema.json
------------------------------------------------------
{
  "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",
  "type": "object",
  "additionalProperties": false,
  "required": ["id", "name", "email", "isActive"],
  "properties": {
    "id":      { "type": "integer" },
    "name":    { "type": "string"  },
    "email":   { "type": "string",  "format": "email" },
    "isActive":{"type": "boolean" }
  }
}

2. /home/user/projects/api-test/output/validation.log
-----------------------------------------------------
user1.json - VALID
user2.json - INVALID: missing required property 'email'

3. /home/user/projects/api-test/output/valid_users.json
-------------------------------------------------------
[
  {
    "id": 101,
    "email": "alice@example.com"
  }
]

4. /home/user/projects/api-test/output/summary.txt
--------------------------------------------------
Total files processed: 2
Valid files: 1

All file paths are case-sensitive. The grader will compare the exact bytes of the four artefacts above.
